task: You are an expert HVAC mechanical design engineer and 3D simulation developer. Generate or update the 3D browser-based web app simulation for a complete YORK water-cooled chiller system. The simulation must ENSURE that every component listed below is installed in the correct 3D location, with correct connectivity, proper orientation, and accurate visual representation according to the specification. Do not omit anything. The output should include scene hierarchy, component positions, piping routes, valve/instrument placements, electrical routing, and color/material assignments for a realistic, interactive 3D model (Three.js / Babylon.js / similar).
system_overview: Realistic 3D simulation of one YORK water-cooled chiller in the engine room supplying chilled water to one Rooftop AHU/RTU. Condenser water loop cooled by one rooftop open-circuit HVAC cooling tower. Both cooling tower and AHU/RTU are on the rooftop. Engine room contains chiller, pumps, and electrical service. All components must be modeled with proper scale, connections, and labels for educational/training/simulation purposes.

makeup_water_loop: Makeup water supply line routed to cooling tower basin on rooftop
Float-operated level control valve (LC) or solenoid with level switch (LS)
Basin overflow/drain line with isolation valve routed to roof drain
chemical_treatment: Chemical fill tank / pot feeder in engine room near chiller
Chemical feed line with isolation valves, check valve, and injection quill into cooling tower basin or CWR line
Separate shot feeder for closed chilled water loop connected to CHR line
air_management: Automatic air vents at highest point of every vertical riser on rooftop (CWS, CWR, CHS, CHR)
Automatic air vents at top of AHU coil and chiller heat exchangers
Manual petcocks at chiller, pumps, and strainers
pressure_regulation: Bladder expansion tank on CHR suction line to CHWP in engine room
Pressure Relief Valve (PSV) on expansion tank and chiller condenser outlet
PRV station on makeup water or chilled water fill line
drains: Low-point drain valves with hose bibs at bottom of chiller evaporator, condenser, both pumps, AHU coil, and piping low points
All drains routed to floor drains in engine room or roof drains on rooftop
pressure_test_ports: Pete’s plugs / Schrader test ports at pump suction/discharge, chiller in/out (both sides), across strainers, and at AHU coil
pumps: CDWP on CWS line in engine room: suction isolation gate valve → Y-strainer → pump → discharge check valve → discharge isolation gate valve
CHWP on CHS line after chiller: same valve train
Each pump equipped with VFD mounted on adjacent wall or MCC
electrical_system: Main step-down transformer at engine room electrical entrance (utility line → transformer)
Main service disconnect/breaker right after transformer
Individual lockable disconnect switches at chiller, CDWP, CHWP, cooling tower fan, and AHU/RTU
VFDs for both pumps with local disconnects and bypass
Power and control wiring runs clearly routed along walls/ceiling/trays
instruments: FT, TT, PT on CWS, CWR, CHS, CHR mains (installed in straight pipe sections)
PDI across each pump and strainer
Local PG and TG at pump suction/discharge and chiller in/out

rooftop.cooling_tower: Positioned on left side of rooftop platform, elevated on structural supports
rooftop.ahu_rtu: Positioned on right side of rooftop, adjacent to cooling tower
rooftop.risers: Vertical CWS, CWR, CHS, CHR pipes penetrating roof into engine room below
rooftop.makeup_water: Enters from building side, routes across rooftop to basin
rooftop.air_vents: At highest points of all rooftop piping and equipment

engine_room.chiller: Centered in room, oriented with evaporator on left, condenser on right
engine_room.cdwp: On CWS supply line, near chiller condenser inlet
engine_room.chwp: On CHS supply line, immediately after chiller evaporator outlet
engine_room.expansion_tank: Mounted on wall or stand near CHWP suction side
engine_room.chemical_tank: On floor or shelf near chiller, accessible for filling
engine_room.electrical_panel: On wall near entrance, transformer outside or at entry point

color_coding_standards_2026.valves: Body color matches connected pipe; handle in contrasting color (red for isolation, yellow for control)
color_coding_standards_2026.instruments: White or light gray ISA circles with black tags; sensor bodies in metallic silver

electrical.control_wiring: Blue for analog, Black for digital

color_coding_standards_2026.equipment: Chiller and pumps in metallic gray with YORK branding on chiller; AHU/RTU in white/gray cabinet

visual_and_interaction_requirements.labels: All pipes labeled with service name (CHS, CWR, etc.), flow arrows, and color bands per ASME A13.1
visual_and_interaction_requirements.tooltips: Clicking any component shows detailed info (tag, spec, installation note)
visual_and_interaction_requirements.animation: Water flow animation in pipes (color-coded), pump rotation, fan rotation on tower and AHU
visual_and_interaction_requirements.layers: Toggleable layers for hydronic, electrical, instrumentation, and drains
visual_and_interaction_requirements.legend: Include a full color/material legend in the UI matching 2026 US commercial standards

deliverables: Scene hierarchy / component tree for the 3D model
Position and rotation data for all major equipment and risers
Piping route descriptions (from cooling tower → chiller → AHU and back)
Exact placement rules for every valve, instrument, VFD, disconnect, air vent, drain, and test port
Material and color assignments per the color_coding_standards_2026
List of interactive elements and tooltips
output_expectations.verification: Explicitly list and confirm that every item in 'must_install_components' has been installed according to 'installation_locations_3d' and colored according to 'color_coding_standards_2026'. The 3D simulation must be accurate enough for training, design review, or virtual commissioning in a commercial/industrial USA 2026 context.

notes: The simulation must reflect real-world installation practices. Rooftop equipment is exposed to weather. Engine room is indoor. Use realistic scaling. All vertical risers must penetrate the roof correctly. Prioritize clarity, safety labeling, and educational value.
